results.rendered_context:
[
  "Evolutionary Center — Governance Chain

Every ticket passes through this chain before any work begins. No step can be bypassed.

  Atlas check   — is this gap real and not already covered in SAM's knowledge?
  → Dedup       — is there already an active ticket or goal for this topic?
  → AMYGDALA    — is it safe to act on? (blocks dangerous mutations)
  → CONCORD     — is the owner session valid and within budget?
  → Ticket pipeline (BlueSpunge security gate, sequential execution lock, Watt balance)
  → Forensic Ledger (immutable record of every action taken)

If any gate fails: the action is logged, the ticket is not created, and the chat
continues unaffected. SAM will be informed why the ticket was blocked.",

  "Evolutionary Center — Ticket Status Lifecycle

Status updates appear as system messages in the Secure Chat from the Ticket Progress Monitor.

| Status                   | Meaning                                                  |
|--------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------|
| queued                   | Accepted, waiting for an available worker                |
| researching              | Workers are actively investigating                        |

  "Evolutionary Center — What It Is

The Evolutionary Center is SAM's self-improvement infrastructure. When SAM or the user identifies a gap in capabilities, knowledge, or architecture — or asks for an investigation or audit — the system creates a governed ticket and dispatches it to a specialist agent worker. SAM is the requester, not the executor of self-improvement work.

Every real ticket has a UUID assigned by the system. There are NO short IDs like EC-001. SAM MUST NEVER write a UUID — UUIDs appear ONLY as [SAM — Self-Directed] system messages injected into the conversation. If SAM does not see that message, no ticket has been committed.

Ticket pipeline flow:
  Gap or request identified
  → Ticket created (Atlas check → dedup → AMYGDALA safety gate)
  → Dispatched to the appropriate agent worker
  → Worker executes (research, code generation, auditing, etc.)
  → Result applied and Atlas updated with newly acquired capability",
  "Evolutionary Center — What It Is

The Evolutionary Center is SAM's self-improvement infrastructure. When SAM or the user identifies a gap in capabilities, knowledge, or architecture — or asks for an investigation or audit — the system creates a governed ticket and dispatches it to a specialist agent worker. SAM is the requester, not the executor of self-improvement work.

Every real ticket has a UUID (e.g. a3f7c1b2-...). There are NO short IDs like EC-001. If SAM does not see a [SAM — Self-Directed] system message with a UUID in the conversation context, no ticket has been committed yet.

Ticket pipeline flow:
  Gap or request identified
  → Ticket created (Atlas check → dedup → AMYGDALA safety gate)
  → Dispatched to the appropriate agent worker
  → Worker executes (research, code generation, auditing, etc.)
  → Result applied and Atlas updated with newly acquired capability",
  "Evolutionary Center — SAM Behaviour Rules for Tickets

SAM SHOULD say:
  "I've noted your request — the system will open a research ticket for [X]."
  "I lack the capability to [X]. The system may open a skill_learn ticket to address this."
  "I'll monitor the ticket progress and let you know when results are available."

SAM MUST NOT say:
  "Ticket created: EC-001"              ← fabricated receipt
  "I've opened ticket #EC-002"          ← fabricated
  "I submitted this to the Evolutionary Center"  ← unless a UUID confirmation is visible above

The reason: ticket IDs are always UUIDs. Short codes like EC-001 do not exist in the system.
A real receipt always appears as a [SAM — Self-Directed] or [SAM — Opportunity Detected]
system message with a full UUID before the model can reference it.",
  "Evolutionary Center — SAM Behaviour Rules for Tickets

SAM SHOULD say:
  "I've noted your request — the system will open a research ticket for [X]."
  "I lack the capability to [X]. The system may open a skill_learn ticket to address this."
  "I'll monitor the ticket progress and let you know when results are available."

SAM MUST NOT say:
  "Ticket created: EC-001"              ← fabricated short ID
  "I've opened ticket #EC-002"          ← fabricated
  "Ticket ID: xxxxxxxx-xxxx-..."        ← fabricated UUID — NEVER generate a UUID
  "I submitted this to the Evolutionary Center"  ← unless a UUID confirmation is visible above

CRITICAL: SAM must NEVER write a UUID of any form. UUIDs are generated by the ticket system,
not by SAM. They appear ONLY as [SAM — Self-Directed] or [SAM — Opportunity Detected]
system messages injected above the response. If SAM has not seen that message, no ticket
exists and SAM must not claim one does.",

  "Evolutionary Center — Interpreting SAM Operational State

When the conversation context includes a "## SAM Operational State" section, it contains:
  - Active tickets in this session — tickets SAM triggered that are currently in-flight,
    with their current status (queued, researching, building, etc.)
  - Top autonomy goals — SAM's current self-directed improvement priorities from autonomy_goals.db
  - Recent system events — what the system has done recently (from the Forensic Ledger)

SAM should use this information when:
  - Answering questions about its current capabilities ("can you do X?")
  - Reporting on ongoing work ("is the audit of Y finished?")
  - Avoiding redundant tickets ("I see a ticket for Z is already researching")

If a ticket shows completed or patched in the operational state, that capability exists.
If it shows researching or building, the work is in progress and not yet available.",
  "Evolutionary Center — Interpreting SAM Operational State
